summary: Gnome Dark Theme Not Respected When "Prefer Wayland" Enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1-stable

### System information

CachyOS (rolling-release) Linux v6.14.1 using Gnome 48 -- Nvidia RTX 4090 FE

### Issue description

When enabling "Prefer Wayland" in `Editor Settings > Run > Platforms > Prefer Wayland` the menu bar "chrome" for Gnome is changed to its light color, instead of dark. (Please refer to the images below).

Prefer Wayland on:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a224ae52-4db1-46d1-87dd-74020bb021e7)
(Credit [adonig](https://github.com/adonig))

Prefer Wayland off:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/41cb7f27-0f50-44aa-a61a-a08de93937f0)
(Credit [adonig](https://github.com/adonig))

Context from @Riteo 

> We have very little control on our current client-side decorations, as we're using a dedicated library for this, libdecor, so the issue is probably there. Judging from the git log they should've fixed it on their main branch with commit [bf091cbf7e](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/libdecor/libdecor/-/commit/bf091cbf7e917fc6ffcb08691d06f5e42daeaa3f) (gtk: Activate dark mode based on DBus) but they didn't make a release with that fix yet.

In short, this should (or is) potentially be fixed in a future update of libdecor (I do not have access to this update quite yet despite being on a rolling release distribution).

It was mentioned that it would be helpful to track this issue in its own report specifically, thus this issue here.

### Temporary Fix

Run Godot on with the following environment variable:

 `GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark ./Godot_v4.4.1-stable_linux.x86_64`. 

Or create a desktop file like the following and put it in `~/.local/share/applications/godot.desktop`:

```bash
[Desktop Entry]
Version=4.4.1
Type=Application
Name=Godot
Icon=/path/to/godot_icon.svg
Exec=env GTK_THEME=Adwaita:dark /folder/path/to/Godot/Godot_v4.4.1-stable_linux.x86_64
Path=/folder/path/to/godot/
```

### Steps to reproduce
1. Running Gnome 47 or Gnome 48.
2. In Gnome: `Settings > Appearance > Style > Dark`
3. Create a new Godot project.
4. `Editor Settings > Run > Platforms > Prefer Wayland (On)`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Reproducible on empty project and completely fresh config files.

summary: [Editor] Use separate `EditorFileDialog` for "Pack Project as ZIP"

description.text:
This prevents state corruption for saving resources.

The alternative solution is to manually set access mode for `file` when used but that feels like a band-aid solution and prone to being missed. Other options like importing templates, exporting mesh libraries, opening and running scripts all have their own `EditorFileDialog` instances so this one makes sense to have as its own.

Only relevant to 4.4 and later as this feature was added in 4.4

* Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/105108

summary: Dropped files replaces `+` character with space

description.text:
### Tested versions

In
v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - SparkyLinux 8 (Seven-Sisters) 8 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (nvidia) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

`Window` class `files_dropped` signal return PackedStringArray of the dropped file path, BUT replaces the plus `+` character with a space, making the path invalid.

I assume it might because of URL-like parsing but in some case having a plus symbol in files and dirrectories is common (e.g. `lost+found` linux directory, a file named for its extended thing, the notepad++ app, etc) so replacing it with space is not the expected behavior.

I expect it doesnt do that, for now i have to rename all the to-be-dropped files to remove the plus character.

### Steps to reproduce

+ Make a directory named
```
dir_!@#$%^&*()_+{}[].,;?'|`~
```
+ Make a file inside it named
```
file_!@#$%^&*()_+{}[].,;?'|`~
```
+ Have a code that do something like:
```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready():
	get_window().files_dropped.connect(on_files_dropped)

func on_files_dropped(files):
	print(files)
```
+ Drop the said file
+ It will output:
```
["/media/game/dir_!@#$%^&*()_ {}[].,;?\'|`~/file_!@#$%^&*()_ {}[].,;?\'|`~"]
```
+ Whereas it should be more like:
```
["/media/game/dir_!@#$%^&*()_+{}[].,;?\'|`~/file_!@#$%^&*()_+{}[].,;?\'|`~"]
```

_(Oh, i just noticed it escapes the single tick `'` but maybe thats for another issue)_

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP for 4.4.1
[issue_drop_path.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19636971/issue_drop_path.zip)

summary: _gui_input ignores z-order and Y sorting, although the documentation says it takes them into consideration

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Tested inv4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
Windows 11
- GeForce GTX 1650

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6 - Vulkan 1.4.303 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650

### Issue description

The documentation on Control nodes state that the _gui_input method filters input based on z-ordering, the control's bounding box and mouse filter, but changing the Z-Order of Control nodes or checking Y Sort on the parent of such nodes doesn't change how events are captured. If control nodes overlap and you generate an event in the overlapped area (for example, a mouse click), the control lower in the scene tree always takes precedence, even if Mouse Filter is set to pass and I don't call `accept_event`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. On a new project, create a 2D scene
2. Place a ColorRect somewhere on the scene and attach a script to it
3. Place another ColorRect somewhere else so that the second rect is overlapping the first
4. Change the z-order of the first ColorRect so it goes back to being infront (optionally, use Y Sorting and place the first ColorRect in a higher Y position)
5. Override _gui_input in both scripts and print something when event is InputEventMouseButton. They must be different messages to differentiate which one got clicked
6. Run the project and click on the first ColorRect in a position that is above to the second one (due to Z ordering).

**What I expected**
- That the message printed was from the first ColorRect, since it is visually in front of the second one

**What happened instead**
- The message of the second ColorRect got printed because it is lower in the scene tree (I assume)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Project already setup for testing: https://github.com/Dancovich/gui_input_test

summary: Unify Scripts Panel naming for the script and shader editors

description.text:
Both use the same shortcut and action, so it makes sense to call it a Files panel as opposed to a Scripts panel.

As an aside, we should see if we can add an alternative shortcut to <kbd>Ctrl + \\</kbd>, as this shortcut is impossible to input on an AZERTY keyboard. The closest equivalent on AZERTY would be <kbd>Ctrl + *</kbd>, but perhaps we should turn it into a physical key binding so it works regardless of keyboard layout. I can look into making this change if desired.

- See https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100287#issuecomment-2607716997. cc @chaosus

summary: Inconsistent calling behaviour of EditorPlugin.resource_saved signal

description.text:
> Edit2: export_custom seems to work (along with custom setters) and @tool-script. This solves my issue, but the underlying question about resource_saved-signal behaviour still remains.


### Tested versions

Occured in 4.4.1-stable (official). Didn't test other versions yet.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Manjaro Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed, 26 Mar 2025 07:48:57 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

I created a custom EditorPlugin to "post process" my custom resources (calculate some values on-save depending on other values etc).

The `resource_saved` signal does not consistently get called on resource save. Here is a simple breakdown:

After editing any value in the resource:
- Using the save button at the top of the inspector dock and chosing `Save` or `Save as...` properly calls the resource_saved signal
- Using the `Save & Reload` or `Save & Quit` button on the `Save modified resources before closing/reloading?` dialog when closing/reloading the project does **NOT** call the resource_saved signal
- Running the game via `Run Project (F5)` or `Run Current Scene (F6)` does **NOT** call the resource_saved signal

For the `Save & Reload`/`Save & Quit` behaviour: I tried printing something on `_exit_tree()` in the `EditorPlugin` to see if it might be unloaded before the resources get saved. But I am pretty sure it is still loaded when the resource gets saved. (Though can't confirm without further testing)

~~I am well aware that this is a non-standard usage. Related but not part of the bug report: I tried using resources with `@tool` and/or with custom setters/getters as well as `_get_property_list()` to show some calculated value but couldn't get it to work. (In my explicit example I want to calculate some sort of "unit cost" depending on their health, damage, speed etc). Optimally as a read-only inspector value (that's calculated either only in the inspector or the inspector calls some special getter function). In my example I really don't need the value persisted and could calculate it on the fly, **I just want a preview of the resulting value.**~~
> Edit: After further testing `_get_property_list()` **DOES** seem to work. Though I am still unsure if I want to have a Resources as as @tool-script. Are there any downsides to this? Any gotchas?

Additionally: None of the EditorFilesystem signals `resources_reload` / `resources_reimporting` / `resources_reimported` / `sources_changed` get called when saving the resource via quit/reload or play. Only the very generic `filesystem_changed` signal gets called. It is also a little bit obscure(?) how the EditorFileSystem signals are different to the EditorPlugin signals (or rather: Why does the raw EditorPlugin have a resource_saved signal but the EditorFilesystem does not?)



### Steps to reproduce

Create an EditorPlugin that listens to the `resource_saved`-Signal.
Create a custom resource.
Edit the custom resource.

Test the different ways to save the resource:
- Save via inspector button -> Signal gets called
- Save via run (current) scene -> **No** signal
- Save via reload/quit project -> **No** signal

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[resource_saved_MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19629165/resource_saved_MRP.zip)

Try editing the `DummyEnemy.tres` located in the root filesystem.

Links to docs:
[Resource Saved Signal (EditorPlugin)](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_editorplugin.html#class-editorplugin-signal-resource-saved)
[EditorFileSystem Signals](https://docs.godotengine.org/de/4.x/classes/class_editorfilesystem.html#signals)

summary: Occluders cast shadows on their own parent canvas items

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot versions 4.4 and 4.4.1

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.4.stable

### Issue description

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d642687e-0837-4e07-8a0e-2bb545290ea5)

As you can see in my image, occluders added to the bottom of wall tiles incorrectly cast shadows over themselves. The intended/desired behavior is for occluders to cast shadows only on other identical wall tiles, not on the same tile. I have been trying for days, but can't find a solution to this problem.

I added an occluder polygon to the bottom of my wall tiles, set the tileMapLayer's canvasItem light mask to 2 and occlusion layer light mask to 2. I have a PointLight2D with item cull mask 1 and 2, and a shadow with item cull mask 2.

The closest solution so far was changing the wall's tileMapLayer's canvasItem light mask to 1, but now the wall normal maps are still affected by the PointLight2D. You can see in my gif this looks very unnatural.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/544089ae-8933-47ae-9a3f-d69282745b89)

So at this point it seems I can either have walls magically illuminate when nearby lights turn on (even without receiving direct light), or walls incorrectly cast shadows on themselves. Unless I'm doing something wrong, 2D isometric shadows are totally impossible and a disclaimer should be added somewhere in the documentation... 

### Steps to reproduce

Use the minimal reproduction project below, it can be opened in 4.4 or 4.4.1, there's no difference.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[example-project1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19625661/example-project1.zip)

### Tested versions

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev (a210fe6db) - macOS Sequoia (15.4.0) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads)

### Issue description

When "Single Window Mode" is enabled, the "hover help" tips in macOS cannot be interacted with: documentation links cannot be clicked, and all mouse input passes through to the editor control below.

This seems to be a side-effect of `EditorHelpBitTooltip` setting flag `Window::FLAG_NO_FOCUS` on macOS.  I get get clickable links when building Godot with the line that sets the flag commented out.

If these are intended to require a modifier to click, pressing any modifier (Command, Control, Option, or Shift) while the popups is displayed dismisses the popup immediately on macOS.

### Steps to reproduce

1. In the Script editor, hover over a documented identifier, (e.g. `_ready` in `func _ready() -> void:` to invoke the popup after a short delay.
2. Attempt to click one of the eight links in the popup.

With "Single Window Mode" enabled, this fails.  With "Single Window Mode" disabled, it works as expected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I experience this with an empty project.

summary: 4.5 Chunked tilemap physics results in large surface count on meshes with holes

description.text:
### Tested versions

Tested in 4.5dev1 mono.

### System information

Linux Mint 22.1 - Godot 4.5dev1 mono - Vulkan (Forward+)

### Issue description

After the new TileMapLayer collision chunking system has been added relatively simple collision polygons I have been using for a terrain are now spamming the error  

`ERROR: servers/rendering/renderer_rd/storage_rd/mesh_storage.cpp:263 - Condition "mesh->surface_count == RenderingServer::MAX_MESH_SURFACES" is true.`

With only a handful of terrain tiles using relatively simple collision polygon shapes using default physics_quadrant_size of 16. The polygon shapes do result in a mesh with a lot of square holes:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19ea3fd1-c12c-4f99-b5ba-ca5ac9a9bb1f)

And after drawing only around 38 tiles I start to get the error that the mesh exceeds the max number of surfaces:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2fded965-bf0f-47a0-aa18-2a9b4dd1c323)

Here are how the polygons are set up in the TileSet:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8b09e511-3625-4e70-85f6-a8a596a21dd1)

HOWEVER if I make one small change to the polygons and delete the extra inside corner vertices:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69069c69-dc1f-46ce-b5fa-ed2e4e984886)

This still results in a mesh with square holes that are rotated 45 degrees and the same number of vertices BUT significantly fewer surfaces in the mesh (not even close):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82483752-9242-4829-9011-7d289574b741)

Because the resulting number of surfaces from the physics chunking is wildly different based on small modifications to the collision polygons (and still very similar shapes/number of vertices) it looks like the algorithm is inconsistent at the moment or at the very least is not well optimized with holes in the mesh. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a TileSet with a physics layer and default value of 16 (or larger) for physics_quadrant_size.
2. Create some tiles using the same physics layer that when placed next to each other will result in a collision mesh with "holes"
in it (in my case fences with corner, cross and t shapes when connected and will result in "holes" when connected in squares).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp---chunking-bug.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19618901/mrp---chunking-bug.tar.gz)

This MRP has a TileMapLayer with view_collision_shapes enabled and collision_visibility_mode set to force shown. It contains a TileSet that has a very simple "waffle" shape and matching collision shapes that result in a mesh that has holes in it as above. I have drawn several sizes of squares filled with the tiles to show how the number of surfaces in the mesh grows exponentially even for very simple collision shapes and exceeds the max number of surfaces with relatively few tiles.

If the square corner vertices are removed resulting in 45 chamfer on the collision polygons as shown below the number of surfaces is drastically reduced even though the "holes" only seem to have rotated 45 degrees:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2be34960-72d3-4c1c-891d-c6695a270522)

summary: Can't use `ResourceSaver.save()` without breaking the UID

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

This is not really a bug, but I think the user expects a way to fix this.

In my project I use `Resource`s to store the settings for my game.
For example, the player may want to save the volume level in the game settings, and that needs to be permanent between a game session and the next one.
The volume level is thus stored in 2 `.tres` files: the default one (can't be changed), and the "latest" one, that is programmatically updated when the player changes the values from the game UI.
Right now this is achieved like this:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	confirm_button.pressed.connect(func():
		latest_settings_data.volume = %Volume.volume
		var path := latest_settings_data.get_path()
		var error := ResourceSaver.save(latest_settings_data, path)
		if error:
			printerr(error)
	)
```
In other parts of the code, i want to load `latest_settings_data` through its UID (because otherwise why has it been introduced?), but only reloading the project I discovered that saving the `Resource` like I did in the previous code basically overrode the UID (or, based on the `ResourceSaver.save()` docs...

> Note: When the project is running, any generated UID associated with the resource will not be saved as the required code is only executed in editor mode.

...probably it just gets rid of it, and then the editor regenerates it later, idk), resulting in a broken unrecognized UID.

I'm sorry if there is already a way to achieve this, but I think that at least the documentation should explain how to handle this.

To fix this, I would expect to be able to do something like this:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	confirm_button.pressed.connect(func():
		latest_settings_data.volume = %Volume.volume
		var path := latest_settings_data.get_path()
                var uid := get_resource_id_for_path(path)
		var error := ResourceSaver.save(latest_settings_data, path, KEEP_UID)
		if error:
			printerr(error)
	)
```
Or I would expect Godot to automatically detect that I'm overriding the file, and the UID needs to be the same.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the `latest.tres` `Resource` file in a text editor so that you can check the content while it get changed.
2. Play the main scene of the MRP project.
3. Tweak the values and then click the *Save* button.
4. You'll notice that the values are correctly serialized in the `.tres` file, but the UID is gone.
5. You can now stop the MRP main scene, and replay it: it will still work (the UID at this point is probably cached, I guess).
6. Instead, if you reload the current project, and invalid UID error is pushed, and you need to tweak with the `latest.tres` file to make it generate another one. Then you should copy/paste the UID again in the `main.gd` file accordingly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[save-resource-uid.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19657560/save-resource-uid.zip)

summary: Controls - overlapping siblings are ignored when passing unhandled input events, causing controls to miss inputs

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1 Windows

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6109) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-6700K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Unhandled input events are passed to parent nodes instead of being tested against sibling nodes that are overlapped by the handling node. In the example, a MarginContainer that is set to full rect logically obscures a button that appears before it in the hierarchy as a sibling. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f90f2bc6-ba35-4211-98b8-42c9001a966d)

The button should be clickable if the obscuring node is set to pass unhandled events, since there is no good reason why the unhandled input event should be skipping any nodes it intersects.

There is [this proposal](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/788) that would resolve this particular case by making MarginContainer default to Ignore since it does not implement any interaction, but this would simply obscure the bug (what about the case of a texturebutton with a mask that reveals the button underneath, and you still want that button to be clickable).

[Possibly related](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/55432) issue.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a CanvasLayer. Create a button. Create a MarginContainer and set it to full rect. Run the scene, note that the button cannot be clicked.

summary: Fix EditorSpinSlider Scroll Edit when inside a ScrollContainer

description.text:
The EditorSpinSlider currently lets users scroll their mouse in order to change the value of the property. However, the EditorSpinSlider does not consume scroll events, so the editor inspector is scrolled at the same time as the value is changed. This makes it difficult to use the scroll button to change the value of a float. This PR changes the behavior to consume the mouse scroll event, so that when the slider is scrolled, the editor inspector does not scroll at the same time.

Additionally, fixes an issue with the mouse warping when grabbing the grabber, where the warping uses local coordinates instead of global coordinates, and so warps the mouse to the top-left of the screen. This PR replaces the local coordinates with global coordinates for the warp.

summary: Exporting @tool scenes with await in @export fields as a resource pack may lose some data.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible: 4.3-stable, 4.4-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Fedora 31 (KDE Plasma) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon(TM) HD8800 Series (PITCAIRN, DRM 3.38.0, 5.8.18-100.fc31.x86_64, LLVM 9.0.1) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4770 CPU @ 3.40GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Suppose I have some table of items (`simple_table.tscn`) and I add some items to it (`simple_item.tscn`). `simple_item.tscn` has an icon and text, looks like this:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/46561312-e3da-4111-a8ce-a760e45d7939)

To make it easy to work with items, I make a `@tool` script and corresponding `@export` fields so that it is possible to externally change the icon and text. 

<details>
  <summary>simple_item.gd</summary>

```gdscript
@tool
class_name SimpleItem
extends PanelContainer

@onready var icon_rect: TextureRect = %"Icon Rect"
@onready var description_label: Label = %"Description Label"

@export var icon: Texture2D :
	set(new_icon):
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		icon_rect.texture = new_icon
	get():
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		return icon_rect.texture

@export var description: String :
	set(new_description):
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		description_label.text = new_description
	get():
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		return description_label.text
```
</details>

From these elements, I make my table:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74fe5c59-cb27-41e9-8b03-42189f57b240)

It's okay, it's working fine in one project. But something strange happens if I want to export scene as resource_pack. And after loading I get:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07361157-0bc0-44fa-a148-196b50591a73)

It's looks like table scene lost icon and text references when exporting. Also, I am sure of this because I used `ResourceLoader.get_dependencies()`. In the first, normal case, it returns two dependencies (`simple_item` and `icon`), but when loading from resource pack it returns only one (`simple_item`). I assume bug happen at the moment of exporting to resource pack. It is strange, because the dependency is already in `.tscn` file. It seems that export calls `@tool` scripts and because of `await` cannot pack them properly. But this is just a guess.

### Workaround

Nevertheless, there is a workaround. I'm creating this issue, among other things, so that people don't waste time searching for a possible solution. In fact, you just need to cache the necessary element in the export field before `await`. And everything will work fine:

<details>
  <summary>simple_item.gd</summary>

```gdscript
@tool
class_name SimpleItem
extends PanelContainer

@onready var icon_rect: TextureRect = %"Icon Rect"
@onready var description_label: Label = %"Description Label"

@export var icon: Texture2D :
	set(new_icon):
		icon = new_icon
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		icon_rect.texture = icon
	get():
		return icon

@export var description: String :
	set(new_description):
		description = new_description
		if not is_node_ready():
			await ready
		description_label.text = description
	get():
		return description
```
</details>

### Steps to reproduce

Simple path:
1. Load `MRP` project.
2. Open `root` scene.
3. Uncheck in inspector `is_load_from_resource` field
4. Start scene. You get expected result.
5. Check in inspector `is_load_from_resource` field
6. Start scene. You get strange result.

`MRP` already has `library.pck` from this project, but you can export it yourself.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug-example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19600886/bug-example.zip)

summary: Godot's LSP does not return correct path for `uid://` resources for `textDocument/documentLink` command.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Not a problem before Godot 4.4

[https://godotengine.org/article/uid-changes-coming-to-godot-4-4/](https://godotengine.org/article/uid-changes-coming-to-godot-4-4/)

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.4.1 Stable

### Issue description

This is linked to the following issue on godot-vscode-plugin:
[https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin/issues/840](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin/issues/840)

Godot's LSP does not return the correct `target` if a resource is referenced using `uid://` scheme, as can be seen below:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fe4e9b2-5277-4252-802a-4633c76557ad)

This causes `uid://` references to break when using VSCode with `godot-vscode-plugin` which i've currently worked around by discarding `uid://` references coming from the LSP.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/13c89fd7-155b-48a3-987e-b360eac501ec)

A potential fix is by overriding `GDScriptWorkplace::get_file_uri` `p_path` with the `res://` path if `p_path` starts with `uid://`, as can be seen in this diff:

(applied to commit [1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/1f56d96cf2c768c3844c68ccb504dfeee841ae15))

```diff
diff --git a/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp b/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp
index b2e52b7425..c3f99cc42d 100644
--- a/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp
+++ b/modules/gdscript/language_server/gdscript_workspace.cpp
@@ -567,6 +567,9 @@ String GDScriptWorkspace::get_file_path(const String &p_uri) const {
 
 String GDScriptWorkspace::get_file_uri(const String &p_path) const {
 	String uri = p_path;
+	if (p_path.begins_with("uid://")) {
+		uri = ResourceUID::uid_to_path(p_path);
+	}
 	uri = uri.replace("res://", root_uri + "/");
 	return uri;
 }
```

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c2baa2be-4323-4b8e-b3e9-2b460076620e)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use godot with VSCode for editing scripts, using [godot-vscode-plugin](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-vscode-plugin)
2. `ctrl+click` on `uid://` references
3. `godot-vscode-plugin` fails to resolve the reference due to the LSP not returning a valid target and shows an error

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

not project dependent, but here you go anyway:
[lsp-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19597990/lsp-repro.zip)
